patches.find: The dome now registers SG null as a prospective prediction (E-PRED-C for the August 12, 2026 eclipse: 'SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal'). A defender could argue this shows advance specification. But this is non-discriminating: standard physics also predicts null SG results during eclipses, since gravitational shielding is not a recognized mechanism. Predicting what standard physics also predicts does not confirm the dome model.
patches.replace: The dome’s current live predictions page (predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) contains no prediction labeled “E-PRED-C.” What it does contain is PRED-TIER-3 (“SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals,” August 2026 eclipse window) — not a null but a positive 10–13 µGal pulse in the very instrument class WIN-014’s 2009 China SG nulls refute. A defender could argue the Aug 2026 eclipse is the real test. But that reframing cuts against this WIN, not for it: the dome is now promising a measurable SG signal in the same instrument class that produced the null it originally claimed as confirmation. The 2009 China SG campaign — continuously sampled across C1/C4 — would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse if one existed. It saw none. The dome cannot simultaneously claim (a) that SG nulls confirm the model (WIN-014, 2009) and (b) that SG gravimeters will show a 10–13 µGal pulse at C1/C4 in Aug 2026 (PRED-TIER-3). Whichever horn holds, the other fails.
patches.rationale: Analyst companion finding (EXP-206): E-PRED-C is a phantom label — WebFetch of dome predictions page 2026-04-21 confirms no such prediction exists. The dome's actual Aug 2026 SG prediction is PRED-TIER-3 (10-13 µGal, not 0.0 µGal). Replacement uses verified PRED-TIER-3 language and pivots to stronger structural argument: dome cannot simultaneously claim SG nulls confirm the model (WIN-014) and predict positive SG signal (PRED-TIER-3).

patches.find: 5×10-5 m/s², per Ferraccioli et al. 2011) widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude.
patches.replace: 5×10-4 m/s², per Ferraccioli et al. 2011) widens the miss by another order of magnitude (to ~17,700×).
patches.rationale: WIN-067.c5 critical hole: unit conversion error. 1 mGal = 10^-5 m/s², so 50 mGal = 5×10^-4 m/s², not 5×10^-5 m/s². Correcting exponent and downstream claim from 'two orders' to 'one order of magnitude'.

patches.find: Closing the c4 advocate’s three-point rebuttal (preemptive). The curmudgeon’s c4 advocate pass (WIN-067.c4.json, defense_survives=4) raises three lines of defense — on terminology, citation numbering, and the 170× ratio. Each folds under the dome’s own live-power page.
patches.replace: Closing three lines of dome defense (anticipated). A dome defender facing our original critique has three lines of retort — on terminology, citation numbering, and the 170× ratio. Each folds under the dome’s own live-power page.
patches.rationale: WIN-067.c5 major hole: opener exposes internal pipeline jargon (c4 advocate, defense_survives=4, GitHub link to .c4.json review file) to public readers. Scientific prose should not reference internal review infrastructure. Replacing with equivalent scientific framing.

patches.rationale: WIN-067.c5 major hole: 170× vs 1,430× headline mismatch. Adding explicit note that 1,430× is the apples-to-apples figure that strengthens the headline 170×, so both numbers can coexist in the text without confusion.

patches.find: the retreat converts this domain — and, symmetrically, every other N/A-tolerance domain — into an unfalsifiable free-PASS that should not count toward the accuracy denominator.
patches.replace: the retreat converts this domain into an unfalsifiable free-PASS that should not count toward the accuracy denominator — alongside any other N/A-tolerance domain that similarly lacks a threshold or categorical criterion (threshold-style panels such as “Anomaly >2σ” are not affected by this critique).
patches.rationale: WIN-064.c5 major hole: sweeping 'every other N/A-tolerance domain' claim invites easy rebuttal via CMB Axis of Evil (which has explicit >2σ threshold and is clearly falsifiable). Narrowing to 'any that similarly lacks a threshold or categorical criterion' preserves the point while removing the over-reach.
